purpose: Self-configuration manifest. An AI agent reads this file once at session start to understand how to use HeurChain for persistent memory, knowledge retrieval, and session continuity. Follow the session_protocol section in order.

deployment.note: All endpoints require no authentication. Replace 192.168.1.203 with your deployment host if different.

interface_selection.description: Choose exactly one interface based on your client type. Do not mix both in the same agent.
use_mcp_if: You are Claude Code or any MCP-compatible SSE client
You can establish a persistent SSE connection to the gateway
use_http_if: You are OpenClaw, Hermes, or any agent making direct HTTP calls
You are ChatGPT or a GPT Action using the REST API
You cannot maintain an SSE connection

mcp.note: heurchain_search uses BM25 ranking over 460+ keys. Prefer it over obsidian_search_notes for knowledge base queries.

http.method: Use standard HTTP GET/POST. No auth headers required.

session_protocol.description: Every agent interaction is a session. Sessions enable context continuity across restarts. Always follow this sequence.

startup_sequence.why: Creates a session_id that namespaces all your working memory. Without this, your memory keys will collide with other agents.

metadata.task: {brief description of what you are doing}
metadata.client: {your client type, e.g. claude-code, openclaw, hermes}

response_fields.session_id: Store this. Pass it to every /agent/store call.

startup_sequence.why: Restores what you knew from prior sessions. If you are a returning agent, this brings back your persistent knowledge and the last session's working memory.

response_interpretation.session_id_null: You are a new agent with no prior sessions. Proceed without context.
response_interpretation.working_empty_longterm_populated: You have persistent knowledge but the working tier was cleared. Review longterm entries.
response_interpretation.both_populated: Full context restore. Treat working[] as recent scratch notes, longterm[] as your durable knowledge base.

startup_sequence.why: Verify all storage backends are available before starting work.
startup_sequence.abort_if: status is not 'ok' or backends.redis.status is not 'connected'

shutdown_sequence.why: Working-tier memory is not permanent. Anything you want in the next session must be explicitly persisted.
shutdown_sequence.how: Call POST /agent/store with persist=true for any key facts, conclusions, or artifacts produced this session.

shutdown_sequence.why: Marks the session as ended and attaches a summary. The summary is searchable in future sessions.

body.summary: {1-3 sentence summary of what was accomplished and what to pick up next time}

key_schema.description: HeurChain uses a colon-separated key namespace. Keys determine which storage tier is used (via 'auto' tier routing). Always namespace your agent-specific keys with your agent_name to prevent collisions.

auto_tier_routing.rule: When tier='auto', the prefix determines the tier.

key_schema.collision_prevention: Always include your agent_name in keys you own. Never write to doc:agent:{other_agent_name}:* keys. Shared keys (doc:proxmox:*, knowledge:*) are readable by all agents but write with care — coordinate with other agents before overwriting shared knowledge.

store_working_memory.description: Store a temporary note scoped to your current session. Cleared when Ori vault is pruned by the consolidation worker (default: 7 days).

store_persistent_memory.description: Store something that must survive session end. Writes to both working tier AND longterm Redis.
use_for: facts learned that apply to future sessions

store_shared_knowledge.description: Write to the shared knowledge base. All agents can read this. Use for infrastructure docs, system state, runbooks.

search.description: BM25 ranked search across all indexed documents (460+ Redis keys + Ori vault files). Use this before answering any question that may already be in the knowledge base.
search.guidance: Score > 2.0 is a strong match. Score < 0.5 is likely noise. If no results, try shorter query terms or fewer words.

get_by_key.description: Retrieve a specific document by exact key. Returns the most recently written copy if the key exists in both tiers.

recall_session.description: Restore the full context of a previous session. Use at startup (step 2 above). Can also be called mid-session to restore a specific earlier session.

optional_params.session_id: Omit to get most recent session. Provide to restore a specific session.

response_fields.session_meta: Session metadata including summary if set
response_fields.working: Array of working-tier entries from this session
response_fields.longterm: Array of longterm entries for this agent

list_sessions.description: List all past sessions for your agent, newest first. Useful for reviewing session history.

get_session_context.description: Get full memory for a specific session by ID.

promote_to_longterm.description: Explicitly promote a working-tier entry to longterm Redis. Useful when you decide mid-session that a scratch note should be permanent.

mcp_tools.description: If using the MCP interface, these are the available tools. Prefer heurchain_search over obsidian_search_notes — it uses BM25 ranking over the full Redis keyspace.

memory.cache_set: Write a key-value pair directly to Redis. No tier routing. For simple ephemeral cache entries.

search.heurchain_search: BM25 ranked search via the broker. Params: query, limit (default 10), tier (all/longterm/working). PREFERRED for knowledge base queries.
search.obsidian_search_notes: Naive substring search across vault markdown files. Use only as fallback.

vault.obsidian_write_note: Write a markdown note to the Obsidian vault. Params: key, content, metadata.
vault.obsidian_read_note: Read a vault note by key.
vault.obsidian_list_notes: List vault notes, optionally filtered by prefix.

health.health_check: Returns status of Redis, Ori vault, and Obsidian vault backends.

behavioral_guidance.description: Decision rules for when and what to store. Follow these to be a well-behaved HeurChain agent.

rules.action: Run the 3-step startup_sequence. Never skip /agent/recall — you may be returning to an in-progress task.

rules.trigger: Before answering any question about the infrastructure, codebase, or domain
rules.action: Call /search?q={topic} first. If score > 1.5, use that context in your answer. Do not hallucinate facts that may already be in the knowledge base.

rules.trigger: You learn a fact that is broadly reusable (IP address, architecture decision, system state)
rules.action: Store to doc:{domain}:{topic} with tier=longterm. Tag with source={your_agent_name}.

rules.trigger: You are mid-task and produce an intermediate result or plan
rules.action: Store to working memory via /agent/store with persist=false. This lets you resume if interrupted.

rules.trigger: Task is complete and you produced a deliverable (code, config, document)
rules.action: Store the outcome with persist=true before session end so the next session can see it.
Call /session/end with a summary. Even one sentence. This summary is searchable in future sessions.

rules.trigger: You are about to write a key that already exists
rules.action: Call /get first. If the existing content is newer or more accurate, merge or append rather than overwrite.

rules.trigger: Your task is specific to your agent and should not be visible to other agents

agent_naming.description: Choose a stable, unique agent_name. This name persists across all your sessions.
agent_naming.convention: Use lowercase hyphen-separated identifiers that reflect your role.

examples.claude-code: Claude Code running in a developer session
examples.hermes: The Hermes/Ori-Mnemos embedding agent on VM 104

agent_naming.warning: Two agents with the same name share session history. Only use the same name if you intend continuity (e.g., always 'claude-code' for all Claude Code sessions).

working.storage: Ori vault (markdown files with YAML frontmatter)
working.persistence: 7 days default (consolidation_worker promotes aged notes to longterm automatically)
working.indexed_by: Keyword search (Ori vault scan), not BM25
working.best_for: Session-scoped scratch space, in-progress notes, short-lived context

longterm.storage: Redis keyspace + Obsidian vault markdown mirror
longterm.persistence: Indefinite (no TTL unless you set one explicitly with /store + redis TTL)
longterm.indexed_by: BM25 in-memory index (rebuilt on startup, updated on every /store)
longterm.best_for: Durable knowledge base, reference documents, agent learning

error_handling.404_on_recall: No prior sessions exist for this agent. Proceed as a new agent without prior context.
error_handling.health_degraded: If ori_vault or obsidian backend is unavailable, longterm Redis still works. Working-tier writes will fail — use tier='longterm' explicitly until resolved.
error_handling.search_empty: No BM25 results means the query tokens do not appear in any indexed document. Try broader terms or check /health to confirm Redis is connected.
error_handling.session_not_found: session_id is invalid or expired. Start a new session. Prior working memory for that session may be unrecoverable.
error_handling.500_on_store: Check /health. Most 500s on /store are Redis connectivity failures or Ori vault path permission errors.
